properties.remark: WALL BLOWN IN AND ROOF BLOWN OFF OF STORAGE SHED AND ONTO CAMPER. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE SUSTAINED TO OBJECTS WITHIN STORAGE SHED. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS ESTIMATED TO BE 80 MPH.
PORTIONS OF THE ROOF WERE REMOVED FROM A BOAT STORAGE FACILITY NEAR KNICKERBOCKER ROAD AND SOUTH CONCHO DRIVE. DEBRIS WAS SCATTERED ACROSS CATTAIL LANE AND STRUCK AN RV.
LARGE TREE UPROOTED.
HAIL BROKE OUT A BEDROOM WINDOW. THE SPOTTER STATED THE HAIL WAS JUST UNDER TENNIS BALL SIZE BUT LARGER THAN THE SIZE OF AN EGG.
A BOAT DOCK IN LAKE BROWNWOOD WAS LIFTED OUT OF THE WATER AND TOSSED ONTO THE BANK. IN ADDITION...THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF OF AN OUTBUILDING AND SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED.
QUARTER TO PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL

A NWS DAMAGE ASSESSMENT TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS BETWEEN 95 MPH AND 110 MPH TOUCHED DOWN. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 185 YARDS AND HAD A TRACK LENGTH OF 2 MILES. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 MILES EAST NORTHEAST OF KATEMCY IN NORTHERN MASON COUNTY AND LIFTED 4 MILES WEST OF FREDONIA IN MCCULLOCH COUNTY.

A NWS DAMAGE ASSESSMENT TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS BETWEEN 95 MPH AND 110 MPH TOUCHED DOWN. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 185 YARDS AND HAD A TRACK LENGTH OF 5.7 MILES. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 MILES NORTHEAST OF KATEMCY IN NORTHERN MASON COUNTY AND LIFTED 2 MILES NORTH NORTHWEST OF FREDONIA IN MCCULLOCH COUNTY.

SEVERAL WATER RESCUES WERE CONDUCTED IN CALLAHAN COUNTY...AS MOTORISTS ATTEMPTED TO DRIVE THROUGH FAIRLY DEEP WATER OVER ROADWAYS. SOME HOMES WERE PARTIALLY FLOODED IN BAIRD. A NUMBER OF COUNTY ROADS WERE FLOODED. A FEW EVACUATIONS ALSO TOOK PLACE IN BAIRD...DUE TO FLOOD WATERS.
LOW WATER CROSSING ARE FLOODED. WATER IS FLOWING OVER HWY 29.

FLOODING WAS REPORTED ALONG PARTS OF FM89 IN TAYLOR COUNTY. WATER LEVEL WAS 1FT OVER THE ROAD IN VICINITY OF LAKE ABILENE STATE PARK. THE STATE PARK WAS EVACUATED. WATER WAS OVER A PART OF HIGHWAY 83/84 SOUTH OF ABILENE IN CENTRAL TAYLOR COUNTY.
